Title: Why Your AI Copy Sounds Like Everyone Else
Body1_Heading: Myth: AI made good enough copy fine
Body1_P1: Here's the deal: when ChatGPT went mainstream everyone had the same idea. Pump out more content. Faster. Cheaper. Good enough is good enough right? The whole industry basically shrugged and said quantity wins now.
Body1_P2: Except the feed didn't get smarter. It got louder. And good enough stopped meaning passable. It started meaning invisible. You know that post you almost published and then read back and thought yeah this could be anyone? That's the problem.
Body2_P1: Two businesses in the same niche can run the same prompt and publish posts that read like twins. Same hooks. Same structure. Same hollow enthusiasm. You could swap the logos and nobody would notice.
Body2_P2: Readers don't scroll past AI because they hate robots. They scroll past anything that feels copy-pasted. And right now most AI content feels like it came off the same assembly line. (Compliments to the chef. Said no one ever.)
Body3_P1: Posting daily with generic copy doesn't train the algorithm to love you. It trains people to skip you. You're not building an audience. You're adding noise to a room that's already too loud.
Body3_P2: One post that actually sounds like you will outperform seven posts that sound like a template every single time. Quality of voice beats quantity of output. Let that sink in. More posts won't fix a voice problem.
Body4_Heading: Instructions will not fix your voice
Body4_P1: Telling AI to sound witty or professional or like a thought leader rarely works. I know you've tried. I have the abandoned custom instructions folder too. We all do.
Body4_P2: It learns from examples not adjectives. Feed it your real writing. A newsletter you wrote. A caption that got real replies. That's how you get output that doesn't make you cringe when you read it out loud.
Body5_P1: A slightly rough sentence in your voice will outperform a perfect paragraph written in generic AI voice. People scroll for connection not grammar scores. They can smell when something wasn't written by a person who gives a damn.
Body5_P2: Robotic polish reads like noise. Your weird phrasing and your actual opinions? That's the stuff people stop for. That's the stuff they save and send to a friend. Pretty good stuff when you let yourself sound like you.
Body6_P1: When everyone has access to the same tools the tool stops being the edge. The edge is you. Your stories. Your phrasing. Your contrarian takes that make someone go wait really?
Body6_P2: Sameness is the enemy. Distinctiveness is the moat. And the flood of generic AI content isn't the threat. It's the opportunity for anyone willing to sound human in a feed full of robots. See?
Body7_Heading: Start with one post this week
Body7_P1: Pick something you wrote that got real replies. Not your most polished post. The one that sounded like you talking to a friend over coffee. (Yes I'm in a coffee shop right now. Where was I? Oh yeah.)
Body7_P2: Paste it into your AI workflow. Generate the next draft from that example. Compare the two side by side. Notice which one you'd actually hit publish on. That's your new baseline. You can do that. I trust you.
Takeaway: STOP CHASING VOLUME. START SOUNDING LIKE YOU.
CTA_Steps: 1) SAVE FOR LATER / 2) COMMENT VOICE FOR MY FREE GUIDE
